summary: Justin Brennan teaches the complete process for acquiring multifamily properties (5+ units) within 90 days by leveraging other people's money (OPM). He breaks down the five pillars: finding deals, analyzing deals, raising capital, closing escrow, and asset management. The core strategy involves identifying off-market deals through broker relationships, structuring deals to minimize personal capital (demonstrated with an 18K down payment on a $2.15M deal), and raising investor equity to control larger properties.
takeaways: Identify 3+ brokers per market, tour on-market properties, then ask brokers for off-market deals - this leverage builds relationships to access off-market inventory where real deals exist
Target 8-20 unit deals as entry point; avoid 50+ unit deals as first investment because you'll lack execution capability
Underwrite deals requiring 14-20% annual returns (IRR) and 6-10% cash-on-cash return - if deal doesn't meet this, don't proceed
Structure capital as 10% GP contribution + 90% raised investor equity; this keeps personal cash minimal while maintaining control and 40%+ profit split
General Partners earn acquisition fees (3% of purchase price) at close and 40%+ of deal profits while investors receive 14-20% annual returns
Lead deal sourcing with: deal → debt (70% bank loan) → equity (30% investor capital), not the reverse; money follows good deals
Use 'soft commitments' by changing your tagline to 'I buy and sell apartments with investors around the country' - this attracts capital before you have a deal ready

skill_candidates.change: Implement the 5-pillar framework (find-analyze-raise-close-manage) with specific return thresholds (14-20% IRR, 6-10% cash-on-cash) to filter deals before capital commitment
skill_candidates.evidence: Brennan repeatedly emphasizes that analyzing deals with these specific metrics prevents pursuing weak opportunities; he states 'if it does not meet that you wouldn't move forward'

skill_candidates.change: Execute 3-broker tour strategy: identify 3+ brokers per market, tour their on-market listings, then request off-market inventory instead of searching listings online only
skill_candidates.evidence: Brennan teaches 'Leverage The on-market to get to the off-market' and shares his Kansas City example: 'I went into that market understood it found three Brokers three properties set up tours for them flew out there... boom that's how we got our first two deals 27 unit a 31 unit'

skill_candidates.change: Start with 8-20 unit deals, not 50+ unit properties; this size attracts responsive mom-and-pop brokers and is capital-executable for beginners
skill_candidates.evidence: Brennan warns 'if you've never done it before you have no chance... you start somewhere between 8 to 20 unit deal the Brokers are more mom and pop they'll actually call you back'

skill_candidates.change: Lead with deal quality first (14-20% returns), then structure debt/equity stack; use acquisition fees and profit splits to minimize personal cash outlay from $100K+ down to $18K-25K on million-dollar deals
skill_candidates.evidence: Brennan demonstrates $2.15M deal with $18K personal capital: '10% is what I bring in... 90% I raised from about five to seven people... acquisition fee for about 46 Grand... so my out of pocket literally was 18K'

title: Own A Multifamily Property In Just 90 Days:  Expert Tips And Tricks! | Justin Brennan
